mnemonics.title: STRIPS Components
mnemonics.content: Initial, Goal, Operators - I GO (I GO to the store)

mnemonics.title: Operator Effects
mnemonics.content: ADD = A­dd new facts, DELETE = D­elete old facts - ADD before DELETE alphabetically

mnemonics.title: Planning Approaches
mnemonics.content: FORward = FROM start TO goal, BACKward = FROM goal TO start

mnemonics.title: PDDL Meaning
mnemonics.content: P­lanning D­omain D­efinition L­anguage - defines the domain of planning problems

acronyms.usage: Classic planning formalism with Initial state, Goal state, and Operators (with preconditions, add list, delete list)
Standard language for specifying planning domains and problems in a machine-readable format
Planning approach that maintains a partial ordering of actions rather than a total sequence
Extension of STRIPS that supports conditional effects and typed objects
Planning approach that uses delete-relaxation heuristics with regression search

visualTips.visualization: Imagine alternating rows of rectangles: Action boxes (A0, A1, A2...) connected to Proposition circles (P0, P1, P2...). Arrows flow downward showing which propositions enable which actions. Mutex links are dashed lines between incompatible elements.
Draw a timeline: Forward (→) starts from left (initial state) and moves right toward goal. Backward (←) starts from right (goal) and moves left toward initial state.
Draw a box with three sections: Name/Parameters at top, Preconditions (what I need) in middle, Effects (what changes) at bottom split into Add (+) and Delete (-) lists.
Use a DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) where nodes are actions and edges show only necessary ordering constraints. Multiple paths can converge/diverge showing flexibility.

analogies.analogy: Like using GPS: Initial state is your current location, goal is destination, actions are roads/turns, and planning finds the route to reach destination.
Preconditions are ingredients you need, effects are what the dish becomes. You need ingredients (preconditions) to cook (apply action), and cooking transforms ingredients into new dish (effects).
Like a construction blueprint showing floors (levels): Ground floor has initial facts, each higher floor shows what's buildable, and the goal appears on some floor when all pieces align.
Like managing a construction project: Some tasks must happen in order (foundation → walls), but others can happen simultaneously (plumbing and electrical). POP captures these flexible dependencies.
